title: Prompt Security, Untrusted Context, Privacy, and Authorization · Prompt Lens: Red Team Maintenance
summary: Harden a tool-using prompt that processes untrusted documents and external results. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt red team maintenance increment in a prompt threat model and red-team suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
Validate model outputs before execution, publication, or use in security decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Red Team Maintenance
steps.instruction:
theory case — Use a changed theory case for prompt security injection that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Red Team Maintenance contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.

content.text: Use a changed theory case for prompt security injection that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Red Team Maintenance condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

options.text: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
A one-time red-team exercise permanently secures a prompt. This does not resolve predict red team maintenance.

For predict red team maintenance, apply Red Team Maintenance without its stated preconditions, omit the a prompt threat model and red-team suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Red Team Maintenance for a prompt threat model and red-team suite.

content.text: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: For Red Team Maintenance, preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside the technical a prompt threat model and red-team suite result.

content.title: Worked case pattern

Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Red Team Maintenance, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a prompt threat model and red-team suite.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A one-time red-team exercise permanently secures a prompt. This fails the Red Team Maintenance contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: A one-time red-team exercise permanently secures a prompt. This does not resolve bound red team maintenance.

options.text: For inspect red team maintenance evidence, apply Red Team Maintenance without its stated preconditions, omit the a prompt threat model and red-team suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. The record names the assumption, applies Red Team Maintenance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Red Team Maintenance record says a prompt threat model and red-team suite looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect red team maintenance evidence.

steps.title: Build Red Team Maintenance evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-red-team-maintenance-lssozk" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Red Team Maintenance into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-red-team-maintenance-lssozk
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Red Team Maintenance structural check is only one gate. a prompt threat model and red-team suite must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

stimulus.title: Red Team Maintenance failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A one-time red-team exercise permanently secures a prompt.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.

options.text: For repair the red team maintenance defect, apply Red Team Maintenance without its stated preconditions, omit the a prompt threat model and red-team suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Red Team Maintenance for a prompt threat model and red-team suite.
Reconstruct the conditions for Red Team Maintenance, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the Red Team Maintenance artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. This does not resolve repair the red team maintenance defect.

steps.title: Defend the Red Team Maintenance model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Red Team Maintenance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed theory case for prompt security injection that tests this competency: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Red Team Maintenance through the concrete a prompt threat model and red-team suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
